249	
250	## Multi-Machine Sync
251	
252	DDx is checked out per-project on every machine an operator uses. During an
253	execute-loop drain, `beads.jsonl` and `executions/` churn every few minutes,
254	and manually pulling/stashing/pushing across machines is friction that gets
255	skipped — leading to divergent tracker state.
256	
257	`ddx sync` is the canonical flow for keeping DDx-managed paths
258	(`.ddx/beads.jsonl`, `.ddx/executions/`, `.ddx/plugins/`) aligned with
259	`origin/main`. Run it as a one-shot (`ddx sync`) or as a daemon
260	(`ddx sync --watch`) that loops on an interval. The command is constrained
261	to the DDx-managed allowlist — it never touches paths outside it — and any
262	condition it cannot auto-resolve (stash-pop conflict, divergent base,
263	double push failure) aborts cleanly, writes a failure record, and surfaces
264	through `ddx doctor`.

input.old_string:
DDx is checked out per-project on every machine an operator uses. During an
execute-loop drain, `beads.jsonl` and `executions/` churn every few minutes,
and manually pulling/stashing/pushing across machines is friction that gets
skipped — leading to divergent tracker state.
input.new_string:
DDx is checked out per-project on every machine an operator uses. While
agents drain the queue, `beads.jsonl` and `executions/` churn every few
minutes, and manually pulling/stashing/pushing across machines is friction
that gets skipped — leading to divergent tracker state.

data.output: The file /tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-abd000f7-20260502T025942-0d468cab/website/content/docs/concepts/architecture.md has been updated successfully.

45	   5. Work is a DAG
46	   6. Right-size the model
47	   7. Avoid vendor lock-in
48	   8. Drift is debt
49	   9. Least privilege for agents
50	   10. Inspect and adapt
51	5. **Principle deep pages** — 10 pages at `/docs/principles/<slug>/index.md`, each rendered from the corresponding `RSCH-NNN` research artifact in `docs/helix/00-discover/research/`. Each deep page presents claim → evidence → DDx-feature mapping.
52	6. **CLI reference** — curated noun-verb command documentation matching the command overview in `CLAUDE.md`
53	7. **Ecosystem page** — shows DDx's position as infrastructure, lists workflow tools built on DDx, highlights HELIX as first plugin. **Dun is removed from all site copy** (no longer a peer in the ecosystem).
54	8. **Server documentation** — how to run ddx-server, MCP endpoint reference
55	9. **Embedded terminal demos** — asciinema recordings of core workflows embedded in hero section and getting-started page:
56	   - Install DDx
57	   - `ddx init` + `ddx list` + `ddx doctor`
58	   - `ddx install helix` (plugin bootstrap)
59	   - One-shot project creation with HELIX
60	   - Feature evolution with HELIX
61	10. **README** — animated GIF/SVG demos, badge row, plugin quick start, link to microsite. The README is the GitHub-facing landing page and must sell at a glance.
62	
63	### Content stance
64	
65	- **Dun removed.** No references to Dun anywhere in site copy, ecosystem diagrams, or glossary.
66	- **Quorum de-emphasized.** Multi-model review (quorum) is no longer a primary capability and is removed from the features list. It is documented only in skill-level pages and badged as "framing" (matches `FEAT-013` status).
67	- **Naming.** `ddx run` / `ddx try` / `ddx work` is the unified primary naming. `execute-loop` / `execute-bead` appear only inside the CLI reference, not in primary copy.
68	- **Software factory framing.** The homepage, `docs/helix/00-discover/product-vision.md` (new "Lineage" section), and `docs/helix/01-frame/principles.md` (new preface) all frame DDx as a document-driven software factory.
69	
70	### Information architecture
71	
72	```
73	/                                  → homepage (sub-claim: "A document-driven software factory.")
74	/why                               → 10 principle summaries (domain principles)
75	/docs/getting-started              → install + first steps
76	/docs/concepts/software-factory    → factory framing
77	/docs/concepts/run-architecture    → run/try/work three-layer model
78	/docs/concepts/<existing>          → architecture, document model, etc.
79	/docs/principles/<slug>/           → 10 deep pages, one per domain principle

ID:       ddx-d532992f
Title:    Investigation/report beads must write evidence to .ddx/executions/, not /tmp
Type:     chore
Status:   open
Priority: 3
Labels:   process, area:agent, kind:improvement
Parent:   ddx-629ec5b4
Desc:     B15a (ddx-8d51b137) wrote its investigation report to /tmp/feat-011-status.md. /tmp is outside the repo, so the post-merge review couldn't see the evidence and flagged BLOCK. Manual workaround: commit 7f943dc1 copied the report into .ddx/executions/20260502T020500-79017d32/. Pattern fix: any bead whose AC says 'output a report' or 'document findings' should write under .ddx/executions/<run-id>/ (or another in-repo path) so the evidence survives reboots and is reviewable. Either: (a) update the execute-bead system prompt to instruct agents to use .ddx/executions/<run-id>/ for investigation outputs, or (b) extend the AC template guidance in CLAUDE.md / SKILL.md to require in-repo evidence paths, or (c) both.
Accept:   1. execute-bead system prompt (cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go executeBeadInstructionsClaudeText/AgentText) instructs agents to write investigation/report outputs under .ddx/executions/<run-id>/ rather than /tmp. 2. CLAUDE.md or relevant guidance file documents the convention. 3. A test or demonstration bead with 'output a report at <path>' AC writes to .ddx/executions/ when executed.

Verification complete. Committed `fbd31a9b`.

**AC results:**
- AC1 hugo build — PASS (no errors, no broken-link warnings; only Hugo API deprecation notices)
- AC2 ddx doc audit — PARTIAL: 90 pre-existing artifact-graph issues (87 duplicate_id from `.agents/skills/` mirror, 2 missing_dep on HELIX/audit docs, 1 cycle); none introduced by the redesign
- AC3 `Dun` grep — PASS (0); rewrote 4 residual spec mentions in FEAT-003 to "deprecated peer tools"
- AC4 `execute-loop|execute-bead` grep — PASS (0); fixed 1 drift line in `website/content/docs/concepts/architecture.md`
- AC5 `quorum` grep — PASS (0)
- AC6 principles dir — PASS (10 slugs)
- AC7 children closed — PASS (22/22 implementation children of `ddx-629ec5b4`; 2 non-closed are this verification bead and `ddx-d532992f`, a process bead filed by review)
- AC8 report — PASS at `/tmp/redesign-verify.txt` and `.ddx/executions/20260502T025942-0d468cab/redesign-verify.txt`
